summary: Matched the small camera accessor function `Camera_80030788` in `src/melee/cm/camera.c`. The function takes a `Vec3*` output argument and copies `cm_80452C68.movement.position` into it. The PR also reformatted an existing `x_dist` expression in `Camera_80029BC4`, apparently as a non-functional style/matching-adjacent cleanup. Evidence is limited to the title, body, and one diff; there were no review comments.

what_changed.details: Replaced the placeholder comment `/// #Camera_80030788` with a concrete implementation: `void Camera_80030788(Vec3* arg0) { *arg0 = cm_80452C68.movement.position; }`.
Reformatted the `x_dist` calculation in `Camera_80029BC4` without changing the apparent expression semantics: division by `cm_803BCB64.aspect * tanf(cm_804D7E60 * movement->target_fov)`.

smart_moves: Recognized `Camera_80030788` as a simple output-parameter accessor/copy from global camera state rather than overcomplicating it with temporaries or field-by-field assignments.
Used the existing typed global camera state, `cm_80452C68.movement.position`, and existing `Vec3` type directly, which keeps the implementation concise and readable.
Linked a decomp.me scratch, `https://decomp.me/scratch/BBLZY`, as external evidence for the match.
decomp_lessons: For tiny camera functions near placeholder markers, check whether the target is just a global-state getter that writes into an output pointer.
Whole-struct assignment of `Vec3` can be the matching and idiomatic solution when the source and destination are already correctly typed.
Existing global camera structs such as `cm_80452C68` can expose enough structure to match accessors without introducing new symbols or headers.
Formatting of nearby arithmetic expressions may be adjusted independently from logic when pursuing clean matched C, but the diff evidence here does not prove it was required for matching.
naming_conventions: Unidentified matched functions retained address-based names such as `Camera_80030788`.
Camera module functions use the `Camera_` prefix.
Camera globals in this area use `cm_`-prefixed address-style names, for example `cm_80452C68`, `cm_804D7E60`, and `cm_803BCB64`.
The output pointer parameter remained generic as `arg0`, indicating no stronger semantic name was established in this PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use direct aggregate assignment, `*arg0 = source_vec3`, for matching a copied `Vec3` result.
Avoid unnecessary local temporaries for small getter/accessor functions when the assembly likely corresponds to a direct copy from a global field into the caller-provided destination.
The PR body points to decomp.me scratch `BBLZY` as the match artifact.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
One issue comment is counted in the metadata, but the only provided human text is the author's decomp.me match link; no reviewer intent or requested changes are evidenced.
follow_up_queries: Look for nearby placeholders `Camera_80030758` and `Camera_800307AC` for similarly small camera accessor/setter patterns.
Search for other functions that copy `cm_80452C68.movement` fields into output pointers to infer better parameter names.
Check whether later PRs rename `arg0` in `Camera_80030788` to a semantic name such as `pos` or `out_pos`.
Compare other `Vec3` getter matches to confirm whole-struct assignment is consistently accepted in this codebase.
